title: ErdŇĎs-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $c>0$ such that in every $H$-free graph $G$ there is a clique or a stable set of size at least $|G|^{c}$.
context_text: ErdŇĎs and Hajnal conjectured this in 1977‚Äď1989; despite considerable attention the conjecture remains open. Until this paper, the only non-trivial prime graphs known to have the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property were $P_4$, the bull, $C_5$, and (in progress) $P_5$.
paper_contribution: Proves infinitely many prime graphs satisfy the conjecture: all graphs $H$ whose every non-trivial prime induced subgraph $H'$ has a vertex of degree one and a vertex of degree $|H'|-2$; more generally, proves it whenever $H_1$ and $\overline{H_2}$ are both buildable (every prime induced subgraph with at least three vertices has a vertex of degree one).
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property

title: Polynomial R√∂dl Conjecture (Conjecture 1.7)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $d>0$ such that for every $\varepsilon\in(0,\frac{1}{2})$, and every $H$-free graph $G$, there is an $\varepsilon$-restricted subset of $V(G)$ with size at least $\varepsilon^{d}\lvert G\rvert$.
context_text: Fox and Sudakov conjectured this polynomial strengthening of R√∂dl's theorem. Taking $\varepsilon$ to be a small negative power of $n$ shows that Conjecture 1.7 implies the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal conjecture. Bucińá, Fox, and Pham [4] later showed the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property is equivalent to being viral (satisfying Conjecture 1.8), which implies Conjecture 1.7.
paper_contribution: The paper's results establishing the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property for new (infinitely many prime) graph classes implicitly verify Conjecture 1.7 for those classes, via the equivalence proved by Bucińá, Fox, and Pham.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property

title: Conjecture 1.8
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $d>0$ such that for every $\varepsilon\in(0,\frac{1}{2})$ and every graph $G$ with $\mathrm{ind}_{H}(G)\leq(\varepsilon^{d}\lvert G\rvert)^{\lvert H\rvert}$, there is an $\varepsilon$-restricted subset of $V(G)$ with size at least $\varepsilon^{d}\lvert G\rvert$.
context_text: This 'polynomial Nikiforov' statement, introduced without external attribution, unifies Theorem 1.6 and Conjecture 1.7. A graph $H$ satisfying it is called viral; Bucińá, Fox, and Pham [4] proved that having the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property is equivalent to being viral.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the ErdŇĎs-Hajnal property
